description: Ice crystals deposited or grown on a cold surface are 'theriko'. Frost may form directly from water vapour or from liquid already on the surface. 'suilo' (dew) remains liquid, while 'kerithe' (ice) names the solid substance more generally.
articulatory_notes: Set the tongue against the upper teeth for unvoiced 'th' and let the breath continue into middle-height front 'e'. Tap or briefly trill the tongue at the alveolar ridge before stressed high-front 'i'. Close the back of the tongue against the soft palate for 'k' and release into middle-height rounded 'o'.

semantic_domains.nature: ice crystals formed on exposed surfaces under freezing conditions
semantic_domains.physical: a thin crystalline deposit of frozen water
